reasoning: The Lean statement captures the universal quantification over both x : ℤ and prime p : ℕ via implicit arguments with hypotheses, matching the informal "any prime divisor" reading. The hypothesis (p : ℤ) ∣ (x^4 - x^2 + 1) exactly encodes "p is a prime divisor of x^4 - x^2 + 1", and the conclusion (p : ℤ) ≡ 1 [ZMOD 12] correctly expresses "p ≡ 1 mod 12". Types (p : ℕ, x : ℤ) are standard and no extraneous hypotheses (e.g., p > 3) are added or dropped. The statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
